jokes.text: We met this other couple at the resort, frank and benny. We hung out with them a lot. Frank and beans. Always makes her laugh. Frank and beans.
jokes.setup: Jim mentions meeting a couple named Frank and Benny at their honeymoon resort
jokes.punchline: He calls them 'Frank and beans' - a juvenile phrase that always makes Pam laugh
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay that establishes Jim's ability to amuse Pam with silly humor. Sets up recurring gag.

jokes.text: Is there someone there? Who is there? I hear voices, is somebody there? Because I'm blind.
jokes.setup: Michael pretends he can't see Jim and Pam who just returned from honeymoon
jokes.punchline: He's doing an elaborate 'blind guy' character bit
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael awkward character work. Fits his need for attention but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Oh, I haven't seen you since my accident that I had when I fell--I fell into the pool of acid, eyes first.
jokes.setup: Michael continues his blind character
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous backstory about falling into acid 'eyes first'
jokes.explanation: The specificity of 'eyes first' elevates this from generic to memorably absurd.

jokes.text: Blind guy. Blind guy McSqueezy. How do I describe it? It is a character I've been workshopping whose lack of vision gets him into all sorts of trouble. The women in my improv class absolutely hate him.
jokes.setup: Michael explains his offensive blind character in talking head
jokes.punchline: He's oblivious that people hate it because it's offensive
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael moment - completely lacks self-awareness about why his character is problematic. Strong cringe comedy.

jokes.text: Oh, 'cause you spent so much on the wedding.
jokes.setup: Jim and Pam only brought back some candy from Puerto Rico
jokes.punchline: Michael passive-aggressively implies they're cheap because of wedding costs
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Michael's inability to filter inappropriate thoughts. Great economy in delivery.

jokes.text: Con quack ulations.
jokes.setup: Dwight formally congratulates Jim on his promotion
jokes.punchline: He says 'con-quack-ulations' because he's giving Jim a duck
jokes.explanation: Silly pun that fits Dwight's character. Sets up the surveillance plot.

jokes.text: I inserted a listening device into the belly of the mallard. Now I can observe Jim, trap Jim, and destroy Jim. Just like in the Bavarian fairy tale. Only this time, the mallard skins the toad alive.
jokes.setup: Dwight reveals his surveillance plan in talking head
jokes.punchline: References obscure Bavarian fairy tale with violent imagery
jokes.explanation: Peak Dwight - combining espionage paranoia with weird cultural references and casual violence.

jokes.text: And of course in this version you lose the whole veiled critique of the kaiser thing.
jokes.setup: Dwight continues explaining his fairy tale reference
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions losing the political subtext about the kaiser
jokes.explanation: Brilliant throwaway line that shows Dwight's mind works on multiple analytical levels even for pranks.

jokes.text: I have recently taken a lover.
jokes.setup: Michael makes formal announcement about his dating life
jokes.punchline: Uses pretentious phrase 'taken a lover'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael pompousness - trying to make casual dating sound sophisticated.

jokes.text: Pam's mom.
jokes.setup: Jim asks who the lucky lady is
jokes.punchline: It's Pam's mom - devastating revelation
jokes.explanation: Perfectly economical bombshell. Two words that change everything. Great setup/payoff structure.

jokes.text: She drives a green Camry. And the seats go all the way down. All the way down.
jokes.setup: Jim demands proof about Pam's mom
jokes.punchline: Michael provides intimate details about car sex
jokes.explanation: The repetition of 'all the way down' makes it more uncomfortable. Classic Michael oversharing.

jokes.text: Jim's horrified reaction and realization
jokes.setup: Michael provides proof about Pam's mom
jokes.punchline: Jim's silent horror as he realizes it's true
jokes.explanation: Great reaction beat. Jim's silent processing sells the horror of the situation.

jokes.text: Get the hell out of here, idiot! What did I do?
jokes.setup: Jim is processing the horrible news about Pam's mom
jokes.punchline: Innocent Toby approaches at worst possible moment and gets yelled at
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - Toby's innocent confusion contrasts with Jim's crisis.

jokes.text: Don't call her 'the mom.' She's right on my way home from work. Then take a different way home, man! I'll take surface streets.
jokes.setup: Jim tries to get Michael to stop seeing Pam's mom
jokes.punchline: Michael's literal interpretation of 'take a different way home'
jokes.explanation: Michael's obliviousness to the real ask. Good character-driven misunderstanding.

jokes.text: Frank and beans. Frank and beans.
jokes.setup: Callback to earlier honeymoon story
jokes.punchline: Jim uses the phrase again to make Pam laugh amid crisis
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows Jim trying to maintain normalcy for Pam during his crisis.

jokes.text: I was probably gonna break up with her anyway.
jokes.setup: Michael agrees to stop seeing Pam's mom
jokes.punchline: Immediately rationalizes with transparent lie
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Michael's ego can't handle being told what to do.

jokes.text: No, no, no, no, no. Oh, no. Oh, my god! Oh, my god! Nooo!
jokes.setup: Michael reveals it's her mother
jokes.punchline: Pam's escalating horror and denial
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction beat. The escalation from 'no' to screaming 'nooo' is perfectly calibrated.

jokes.text: I honestly thought that could've gone one of two ways, but I never expected her to get upset.
jokes.setup: Pam has just had a complete breakdown
jokes.punchline: Michael's oblivious analysis that he didn't expect her to be upset
jokes.explanation: Peak Michael delusion. The understatement after Pam's meltdown is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: There's a knife in your back and the blood is gushing down. I'm sleeping with Pam's mom. Sometimes dinner.
jokes.setup: Creed is doing some weird relaxation thing with Michael
jokes.punchline: Michael casually drops his confession during the weird ritual
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of Creed's weird imagery with Michael's confession. 'Sometimes dinner' is perfect detail.

jokes.text: You know, I really would've appreciated a heads up that you were into dating mothers. I would've introduced you to mine.
jokes.setup: Michael confesses to sleeping with Pam's mom
jokes.punchline: Creed's disturbing offer to introduce Michael to his own mother
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed moment - takes the situation to an even darker place with casual delivery.

jokes.text: So I think if I played it just right, I can get Dwight to live out the plot of National Treasure.
jokes.setup: Jim found the listening device in the duck
jokes.punchline: Plans to manipulate Dwight using elaborate movie plot
jokes.explanation: Great Jim prank concept. The specificity of National Treasure makes it funnier.

jokes.text: DWIGHT PICKED THE WRONG DAY TO PUT A WOODEN MALLARD IN MY OFFICE.
jokes.setup: Jim's personal crisis with Pam's mom
jokes.punchline: He's going to take out his frustration on Dwight through elaborate prank
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Jim's displaced anger. The dramatic emphasis sells his emotional state.

jokes.text: Last Sunday I had to put down over 150 pets all by myself.
jokes.setup: Dwight volunteers at animal shelter
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions mass euthanasia as if it's normal volunteer work
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight dark humor delivered matter-of-factly. The number '150' makes it absurd.

jokes.text: I have a way to make Scranton a better place. You could leave it.
jokes.setup: Meeting about helping the community
jokes.punchline: Stanley suggests Michael leaving would help Scranton
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley deadpan. Simple but devastating put-down delivered with economy.

jokes.text: Pamela Morgan Beesly, you need to apologize to your mother right now.
jokes.setup: Michael takes phone call from Pam's mom
jokes.punchline: Uses Pam's full name like she's a child in trouble
jokes.explanation: Michael completely overstepping boundaries, treating employee like his stepdaughter.

jokes.text: For trying to find happiness in the arms of a lover? Don't call my mother your lover!
jokes.setup: Michael defends his relationship
jokes.punchline: Pam's revulsion at the word 'lover'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation. Michael's pretentious language makes it worse for Pam.

jokes.text: I don't think there's a whole lot of sleeping going on.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Michael 'sleeping' with Pam's mom
jokes.punchline: Kevin's crude implication about what they're actually doing
jokes.explanation: Classic Kevin crude humor. Predictable but fits character perfectly.

jokes.text: I may someday be your father, so get out. You are never gonna be my father. I hope that you are willing to die in this office, because I am.
jokes.setup: Michael pulls rank as boss
jokes.punchline: Threatens to be her stepfather, leading to dramatic standoff
jokes.explanation: Great escalation to absurd workplace standoff. The 'die in this office' threat is perfectly ridiculous.

jokes.text: My mother's in a wheelchair. Well, he could still...
jokes.setup: Oscar explains his mother couldn't date Michael
jokes.punchline: Kevin starts to suggest Michael could still have sex with disabled woman
jokes.explanation: Dark Kevin humor that goes too far, hence the cut-off. Pushes boundaries appropriately.

jokes.text: Where did you get your fedora? I'd rather not say. You think I'm gonna get the same fedora as you?
jokes.setup: Michael wants fashion advice from Ryan
jokes.punchline: Ryan's paranoid secrecy about his hat source
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ryan's pretentious gatekeeping of fashion.

jokes.text: If you take it back I'll scream. I'll give you five bucks for it. Twenty. Ten. Deal.
jokes.setup: Dwight tries to get the mallard back from Kelly
jokes.punchline: Kelly's dramatic threat followed by immediate capitulation for money
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kelly moment - dramatic threat immediately abandoned for small financial gain.

jokes.text: That's why they pay me the big bucks.
jokes.setup: Toby agrees to help with Michael's HR complaint
jokes.punchline: References his famously low salary as 'big bucks'
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating Toby humor. Everyone knows HR doesn't make much money.

jokes.text: Okay, you're a jackass.
jokes.setup: Michael complains about not being able to make Pam leave
jokes.punchline: Mild-mannered Toby finally snaps and insults Michael
jokes.explanation: Great character moment. Toby never insults anyone, so this carries real weight.

jokes.text: You're just as stubborn as your mother. When you don't want to do something, you just don't do it. Michael, you're just her rebound!
jokes.setup: Escalating argument between Michael and Pam
jokes.punchline: Pam delivers devastating personal attack about being a rebound
jokes.explanation: Great escalation. The rebound comment is a perfect personal attack that hits Michael's insecurities.

jokes.text: Frank and beans...
jokes.setup: Tense moment after Pam's revelation about Michael
jokes.punchline: Jim weakly tries the familiar joke in completely inappropriate moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that shows how inadequate their usual comfort mechanisms are in crisis.

jokes.text: I could give a crap about your happiness! Stop dating my mother! You know what? I'm gonna start dating her even harder. What's that supposed to mean?
jokes.setup: Pam demands Michael stop dating her mother
jokes.punchline: Michael's threat to 'date her even harder' - nonsensical but threatening
jokes.explanation: Michael's childish threat is absurd but somehow menacing. 'Even harder' doesn't make sense but sounds bad.

jokes.text: What's her face from Quiznos. I see her four times a week.
jokes.setup: Michael continues listing his 'female friends'
jokes.punchline: Counts service worker he doesn't know as friend
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael delusion. Doesn't even know her name but considers her a friend.

jokes.text: Just to be clear, you're terrible at this, and you're not equipped for espionage. Oh, I'm equipped. I can es— Nope.
jokes.setup: Jim critiques Dwight's spy skills
jokes.punchline: Dwight starts to argue but Jim cuts him off dismissively
jokes.explanation: Good Jim/Dwight dynamic. The 'nope' cutoff is perfectly dismissive.

jokes.text: So Dwight heard you were having a really rough day, so he generously offered to wash our car. Aww, he did that for me? Yes. He did.
jokes.setup: Jim tells Pam about Dwight washing their car
jokes.punchline: Pam thinks it's sweet, not knowing it's punishment for spying
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection. Pam's genuine appreciation contrasts with the real reason.
